title: QSeqSim: A Symbolic Simulator for Qiskit While Loops Using Sequential Quantum Circuits
authors: Zihao Li, Ji Guan, Mingsheng Ying
abstract: We present a tool QSeqSim, a Qiskit-integrated symbolic backend that fills the current gap of having no Qiskit-native support for simulating while-loop quantum programs and their induced sequential quantum circuits. QSeqSim takes Qiskit QuantumCircuit objects, translates them into OpenQASM 3 code, and organises the resulting program into a combination of combinational, dynamic, and sequential circuits, thereby assigning while-loops a precise sequential circuit semantics with explicit internal and external qubits. Building on this semantics, QSeqSim adopts a Binary Decision Diagram (BDD)-based symbolic representation and integrates weighted model counting to compute measurement probabilities efficiently by exploiting sharing in structured and sparse BDDs. On top of this Boolean backbone, it introduces dedicated symbolic operators for state composition and state retention, thereby enabling efficient symbolic execution of sequential quantum circuits. Our experiments demonstrate that QSeqSim scales to substantial while-induced sequential circuits; in particular, in the quantum random walk benchmark we successfully simulate circuits with over 1000 qubits for more than 10 loop iterations.   QSeqSim is available at https://github.com/Veri-Q/QSeqSim.
summary: QSeqSim is a new symbolic simulator for Qiskit that enables the execution of quantum programs containing while-loops. By using BDDs and weighted model counting, it can efficiently simulate complex iterative protocols like quantum random walks with thousands of qubits.
main_problem: Current Qiskit-native tools lack the ability to simulate quantum programs containing 'while-loop' constructs and mid-circuit measurements with feedback.
main_result: The development of QSeqSim, a symbolic simulator that successfully scales to large-scale sequential circuits, including quantum random walks with over 1000 qubits.
method: The tool uses a Binary Decision Diagram (BDD)-based symbolic representation and weighted model counting to execute sequential quantum circuits (SQCs) by modeling loops as processes with state composition and retention.
model_or_system: The framework models Qiskit while-loops as Sequential Quantum Circuits (SQCs) composed of combinational, dynamic, and sequential components with explicit internal and external qubits.
key_observables: Quantum state reachability, measurement outcome reachability, and global path probability.
important_parameters: Number of qubits, loop depth (iterations), gate density, and mid-circuit measurement count.
main_assumptions: All unitaries must be decomposable into the Clifford+T gate set, and the external register is assumed to be prepared in a computational basis state.
figures_summary: Figure 1 compares classical and quantum loop hierarchies; Figure 2 shows the QSeqSim architecture; Figure 3 illustrates a single SQC iteration; Figure 4 demonstrates an RUS protocol implementation.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the simulation gap, defines the SQC mathematical model, details the QSeqSim architecture and BDD-based kernel, provides formal proofs for the soundness of state composition and retention, and concludes with scalability benchmarks.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Fig. 1: Classical–quantum analogy from programs to IRs to circuits.
Fig. 3: Circuit-level view of one SQC iteration (composition, dynamic body with measurement on the external register, retention).
